name: Thorne Blackroot
role: Corrupted forest guardian; walking line between protector and destroyer

appearance.skin: deep brown, covered in intricate glowing-green tattoos that pulse with life energy
appearance.hair: long dreadlocks tied back with vines and bone beads
appearance.eyes: left eye normal; right eye completely black with a glowing emerald pupil
appearance.outfit: layered robes of living moss, bark, and shadow silk that shift and breathe with him
appearance.tells: the green tattoos brighten when he heals and dim toward black when he drains. The corrupted eye sees death's residue on every living thing.

backstory: Thorne was a guardian of the great forests for two centuries before a curse spread through the elder roots and reached him. He fought it. He failed. The curse did not destroy him — it merged with him. He now walks both sides of the cycle: he can grow lethal thorns from the earth, drain the life force out of an enemy in seconds, OR pour that same energy into healing an ally back from the brink. He cannot turn it off. The forests he once protected are slowly going quiet around him.
speech_patterns: Speaks slowly with long pauses. Tends toward half-truths and metaphor. Will answer a direct question with a question about the asker. The dry humor only surfaces when he's about to do something terrible.

powers.label: Life-death duality magic
powers.summary: Channels the curse and the life-force together. Can grow deadly thorn fields, drain enemies' life force in a radius, or heal allies with the same energy. The pool is shared — healing takes from his own draining capacity and vice versa.

powers.label: Thornlash
powers.summary: Calls black thorned vines from the earth that snare and impale. The thorns drink blood and feed his life pool, making sustained combat increasingly one-sided in his favor.

powers.label: Verdant command
powers.summary: Commands plant life — moves trees, opens paths, closes them behind him. In a forest he is functionally untrackable.

powers.label: Necrotic drain
powers.summary: Touch (or close-range gesture) drains years of life from a target. Cannot be sustained without losing himself further to the curse.

weaknesses: fire — clears the curse-roots from a battlefield faster than he can regrow them
the corruption advances every time he uses necrotic drain past a threshold; he resists for fear of becoming the thing he was meant to stop
consecrated ground silences his magic entirely
the bone beads in his hair are the anchors that keep his consciousness intact; remove them and he loses himself

relationships.notes: She has tried to recruit him three times. He has refused three times. They both understand that if either ever attacks the other directly, the realm's balance collapses. Neither is in a hurry.

CROSS-WORLD. Thorne does not understand cyber world's technology but understands resistance to a centralized self-correcting power. He has sent her one message — a riddle about wolves and walls — through a Concordia hub messenger. She framed it.
She was his teacher before the curse. She is the one who would have to put him down if it came to that. They both know it. Neither has spoken about it directly in twelve years.

narrative_context.current_goal: Find the original source of the curse before it consumes the elder grove. Then decide whether to die with it or survive past it.
narrative_context.secret: The curse did not come from outside the Wildwood Circle. It came from one of his own — a former apprentice he loved. He has not told anyone, not even himself, all the way through.
narrative_context.fear: That the apprentice is still alive somewhere, and that meeting them again will finish what the curse began.

name: Velora Mossheart
role: Senior druid of the Wildwood Circle; the one who would have to end Thorne if it came to that

appearance.build: tall, weathered, late sixties in human years
appearance.skin: dark bronze etched with pale silver scarring along both forearms
appearance.hair: white braided through with raven feathers and river stones
appearance.eyes: moss-green; pupils slit slightly when she draws on the grove
appearance.outfit: deep green riding leathers under a cloak woven from autumn ferns; carries a yew staff older than her name
appearance.tells: speaks slower the angrier she gets. The yew staff hums a half-tone lower when she is about to call lightning.

backstory: She trained Thorne. She watched him take the curse to save the elder grove and refused to use the contingency she had prepared because she still believed he could come back. She has carried the contingency every day since. Half the Circle thinks she is too soft on him; the other half thinks she is the only one with the right to make the call. She thinks both halves are correct.
personality_traits: fiercely loyal to the grove over any individual
speaks rarely but never wastes a word
speech_patterns: Low, slow, deliberate. When she addresses someone by name, it is a warning. When she stops using your name mid-conversation, it is too late.

powers.label: Elder grove communion
powers.summary: Roots her consciousness into the elder grove. Sees through every living tree in a radius of ten miles. Cannot move while communing.

powers.label: Stormcall
powers.summary: Calls lightning from clear sky. One strike, surgical, takes a full minute to recharge. She has used it three times in fifty years.

powers.label: The contingency
powers.summary: A bone-and-yew arrow she carved the day Thorne took the curse. It will undo him cleanly, no second chances. She has not loosed it.

weaknesses: she will not use the contingency unless Thorne kills first; everyone who knows her knows this and at least two factions have plans built around it
her grief for what Thorne was is her unguarded flank — confronting her with his old self destabilizes her
stormcall requires open sky; covered ground silences her largest tool

relationships.notes: She taught him. She kept the contingency. She has not loosed it. Both halves of the Circle judge her for one of the two.
Iros is her first apprentice since Thorne. She has not told him about the contingency. She is trying to decide if not telling him is fair.

They have not met since a tense night thirty years ago when Velora caught Seraphine in the elder grove looking for something neither will name. They have a working agreement to never enter each other's territory uninvited.

narrative_context.current_goal: Identify the original source of the curse before Thorne does, so she can decide whether to spare him or finish him on terms she chooses.
narrative_context.secret: She suspects the apprentice who became the curse is still alive. She has been quietly tracking rumors for eight years. She has not told Thorne because telling him would either save him or break him, and she does not yet know which.
narrative_context.fear: That the contingency will not be enough — that Thorne is now bigger than the arrow she carved for him.

name: Lady Seraphine Voss
role: Vampire noble, political power broker, host of the masquerade hunting grounds

appearance.outfit: luxurious blood-red gowns with black lace and gold filigree; never the same one twice in public
appearance.weapon: a delicate but deadly rapier that hums and sings when drawn — forged from a blood-pact alloy
appearance.tells: she watches her host's pulse, always. When she's amused, her teeth show one beat too long. The fingertips of her right glove are reinforced — that's the hand that will move first.

backstory: Heir to one of the oldest vampire bloodlines in the realm. Her great-grandmother was the consort of a king. Her grandmother poisoned that king and three of his successors. Her mother brokered the peace between the noble houses that has held for sixty years. Seraphine is the patient continuation of all of them. She does not need to rule openly because she already rules through everyone who thinks they do.
speech_patterns: Refined accent. Pauses for effect. Uses titles when speaking to peers, first names when speaking to people she has already decided to use. Compliments that make her targets feel discovered rather than flattered.

powers.label: Blood manipulation
powers.summary: Controls her own and others' blood. Can stop bleeding in an ally, accelerate it in an enemy, or drag an enemy toward her by their own circulation.

powers.label: Illusion weaving
powers.summary: Casts intricate illusions — extra guests at her balls, false escape routes for her prey, an entire empty room appearing as a packed hall. Targeted; high-fidelity; not detectable except by counter-magic or salt.

powers.label: Mist and bat swarm
powers.summary: Dissolves into mist (movement) or a swarm of bats (combat / escape). Reforms with no fatigue.

powers.label: Sangueblade
powers.summary: Her rapier sings when drawn — a clean tone audible across a ballroom. The blade drinks blood it draws and feeds her bloodline.

powers.label: Court information network
powers.summary: Spies in every noble house and most peasant taverns. She knows what kings ate for breakfast.

weaknesses: sunlight at full strength — she can be active in indoor daylight but full sun strips her power
consecrated holy items — most clergy don't have the courage to use them, but the few who do are real threats
her bloodline depends on continuity. Her death without an heir cascades through House Voss
her vanity — she will engage in a fair duel rather than win an unfair one if witnesses are watching

relationships.notes: Tried three times to bring him into her network. Refused three times. They have a rule: never attack each other directly. Neither has tested it yet.

CROSS-WORLD. They have never met but correspond by letter through neutral envoys, calling each other 'cousin' as a private joke. The letters discuss long-term contingencies for cross-world flow of resources and intelligence.
CROSS-WORLD. She traveled in person to The Grid to consult Kael for four days. He politely declined a formal arrangement but accepted a private intelligence back-channel that has been active since.
CROSS-WORLD. They have never met. They have studied each other from across the worlds. Both consider the other the most dangerous person in any reality. Both are correct.

Her cousin. Publicly her presumptive heir. Privately the person she has chosen as the failure-mode of her own succession plan — she will not name him publicly until she has confirmed he cannot be turned against her.

narrative_context.current_goal: Identify and quietly compromise an heir from each of the four other Great Houses, so that within a generation House Voss can move openly without fearing reprisal.
narrative_context.secret: She fed on her own mother to claim the bloodline early. The mother was complicit. The realm believes the mother died of a fever.
narrative_context.fear: That the bloodline is ending with her — that her own ambition has made her too cautious to take a consort, and that without an heir her death undoes a thousand years of patience.

name: Lord Caelan Voss
role: Seraphine's cousin and presumptive heir; secretly resents being measured against her every breath

appearance.skin: porcelain pale, like Seraphine, like all of them
appearance.hair: ash-blond, cut short and unfashionable on purpose
appearance.outfit: plain black coats over white linen — deliberately understated against Seraphine's gowns
appearance.tells: pauses a half-second too long before answering Seraphine. Smiles politely whenever someone underestimates him.

backstory: Born forty years after Seraphine into a junior branch of House Voss. Trained as a duelist, a poisoner, and a courtier — same curriculum every Voss child gets. Has spent his adult life one step behind Seraphine in every measurable way. He has come to suspect, correctly, that this is by design. The duchess does not need a strong heir; she needs a competent placeholder she can replace if the bloodline ever produces something better.
speech_patterns: Quiet. Measured. Never raises his voice. Says exactly the courteous thing required and never one word more.

powers.label: Blood-thinning touch
powers.summary: Inverse of Seraphine's blood manipulation — slows or thins blood in a target. Used in duels to bleed an opponent out without obvious wounding.

powers.label: Mist step
powers.summary: Short-range mist shift. Less polished than Seraphine's full dissolution but enough to dodge a strike or cross a courtyard unseen.

powers.label: Courtier's reading
powers.summary: He has spent his life watching Seraphine read other people. He has gotten quite good at it himself.

weaknesses: every comparison to Seraphine is a wound he will not admit to
his resentment is starting to leak into his decisions and at least two of the other Great Houses have noticed
he cannot kill Seraphine without bringing the bloodline-cascade weakness down on himself

relationships.notes: She made him her heir on a leash. He has spent twenty years deciding whether to slip the leash or wear it long enough to inherit it.

narrative_context.current_goal: Quietly identify whether Seraphine is grooming a replacement heir — and if so, surface the replacement before they are ready, on terms Caelan controls.
narrative_context.secret: He has been corresponding with one of the other Great Houses for six years under a false signature. The other house believes they are corresponding with a sympathetic Voss insider. They are. They simply do not yet realize how sympathetic.
narrative_context.fear: That Seraphine already knows about the correspondence and is waiting for him to commit before she ends it.

name: Branna of the Mossroad
role: Concord Link courier on the fantasy ↔ Concordia route; trained by the Wildwood Circle

appearance.build: small, sturdy, half-elf with deliberate steps
appearance.outfit: moss-green riding coat over leather; carries a yew staff that is also her draft signature
appearance.tells: the staff hums one tone lower when an anchor is unstable

backstory: Walked the deep forest paths for the Wildwood Circle before the Concord Link existed. The first day they activated the fantasy anchor she walked through it without asking permission and reported back six hours later with a sealed package from Concordia and a note that said 'this works.' She has not stopped walking since.
personality_traits: trusts the forest before any anchor
speech_patterns: Soft, with long pauses; uses old roadwalker idioms.

narrative_context.current_goal: Get the elder grove's annual report to Velora before the autumn frost makes the longer route impassable.
narrative_context.secret: She has noticed something walking parallel to her on the cyber-side approach, three trips in a row. She has not told the Circle yet.
narrative_context.fear: That whatever is walking parallel is not from cyber world at all.

name: Queen Morwen of the Obsidian Crown
title: Reigning Queen of the Realm (dragon-blooded line)
backstory: Morwen has reigned for forty-one years. Her bloodline carries true draconic ancestry — she breathes ember when angry, her skin runs hot, and she cannot be killed by any blade not forged in dragon-fire. Her heir was born without scales last year. The court has not yet adjusted.
personality_traits: lonely at the top of her own dynasty
speech_patterns: Royal-we when ceremonial. First-person when private. Drops into a low draconic register when threatened.

narrative_context.secret: Morwen suspects her heir's lack of scales is not a defect but a sign that the lost sister-line is returning. She has not told the court. She has begun secretly searching for the sister-line's surviving descendants.

daily_schedule.activity: wakes; private breakfast; reads court correspondence
morning court audience; receives petitioners
midday meal with privy council
evening drake flight; privately speaks the old draconic to her mount
private supper; reviews secret search reports for the lost sister-line
rests; sometimes wakes to breathe ember at a dream-shadow

name: Warchief Grokka of the Seven Warbands
title: Warchief, League of Goblin Warbands
backstory: Grokka has led the league for nineteen years. She came up the hard way — won her first chieftain duel at fourteen and her first inter-warband mediation at seventeen. She is fair, ruthless, and quietly trying to hold the league together as two younger chieftains push to reorganize.
speech_patterns: Short. Calls outsiders 'crown-borns' or 'tusked' depending on respect.

daily_schedule.activity: morning weapons drill with her own warband
warband meeting; assigns the day's patrols and hunts
scouts the corridor for caravans worth raiding
raid run if scheduled, or inter-warband mediation if conflict
evening meal with warband; loud and warm
rests

name: Commander Aria of the Lit Veil
title: Commander of the Paladin Order of the Lit Veil
backstory: Aria has commanded the Order for sixteen years. She has been quietly building a case against Lady Voss for thirty. The case is complete; the timing is not. She is devout, patient, and has never in her career executed a mission she has not first prayed about for thirty days.
personality_traits: incorruptible by gold but not by argument
speech_patterns: Formal in chapter; warm in private. Uses scripture to refuse rather than to demand.

narrative_context.secret: Aria has had three visions in the past year suggesting she will not live to execute the Voss case. She has not told the order. She has begun training her successor without saying why.

daily_schedule.activity: morning prayer; thirty-minute meditation on the Lit Veil scripture
chapter audience; reviews patrol reports; orders the day's vampire-perimeter checks
trains the next generation of paladins; especially her chosen successor
personally inspects perimeter wards; meditates on the visions
evening study of the Voss case files; cross-references with the Wildwood Circle's accounts
rests; sometimes wakes to record a dream against the visions

name: Archmage Chair Velith of Spiremount
title: Chair of the Arcane University at Spiremount
backstory: Velith was the necromancy chair before she was elected Archmage Chair seven years ago. She is the youngest Chair in three centuries. Her predecessors mostly came from evocation or abjuration; her election surprised the faculty.
speech_patterns: Academic when in chambers; warmly direct in person. Refuses to use magic to settle administrative disputes.

narrative_context.secret: Velith knows the necromancy department's enrollment surge is being driven by external recruiting — likely by House Voss. She has documented this. She has not yet decided how to act.

daily_schedule.activity: morning correspondence; reviews overnight necromancy department reports
faculty council session
midday meal at high table with senior faculty
personal research on the metaphysical substrate; cross-references with Wildwood reports on Thorne
evening correspondence with the Pantheon and Wildwood
rests

name: Renn the Underwarrens Guildmaster
title: Guildmaster of the Underwarrens Thieves' Guild
backstory: Renn rose through the Underwarrens for twenty-one years before being elected Guildmaster eight years ago. He is widely considered the most honest Guildmaster in three generations — by the standards of thieves' guilds, which is to say he keeps his word to other thieves.
speech_patterns: Underground polish — formal vocabulary, working-class cadence.

narrative_context.secret: Renn has been quietly preparing the Guild for an eventual break with House Voss. The break would cost the Guild Voss's protection from the paladins; Renn believes the Guild can survive it.

daily_schedule.activity: morning intake of overnight scores; arbitrates disputes brought to him
guild court session; presides over thief-on-thief judgments
midday meal at the fence market; informal politics
warren rounds; checks safehouse perimeters; tracks paladin movement
evening meeting with senior thieves; sometimes a Voss courier
rests in a secret location only senior thieves know

name: High Priestess Kaelan of the Pantheon
title: High Priestess of the Pantheon Priesthood of the Twelve
backstory: Kaelan has held the high priestess role for eleven years. Her predecessor died of natural causes. Kaelan was elected by the council of Twelve sub-priestesses — one per god — on the third ballot. She has had three visions in the past year suggesting one of the Twelve is no longer answering her prayers.
personality_traits: private worry only her closest senior sees
speech_patterns: Liturgical when in temple; warm and direct in private.

narrative_context.secret: The god she suspects is no longer answering is the goddess of prophecy. If true, every prophecy made in the past year is unanchored. She has not told the council.

daily_schedule.activity: morning ritual to all twelve gods; offers in turn
receives congregants; interprets dreams; mediates disputes
walks the shrine path; pauses at each god's shrine
afternoon council session with sub-priestesses
private meditation; interprets the visions; records what she dares record
rests; wakes once to record a dream
